notes: Subwindows are derived from review contact sheets, not committed source video clips. Frame numbers use nominal 25 fps source timing. This source is currently the strongest active-gameplay Galaxian reference in the local library.

subwindows.tile_cadence: 4 fps sheet uses 0.25 second cadence, left-to-right then top-to-bottom.
observations: Clip opens on a settled active rack rather than an attract/game-over presentation.
Player Galaxip is visible in the lower playfield with reserve ships at the bottom.
Early enemy dives and projectiles are already present.
subwindows.implementation_use: Good initial active-play window for formation state, player lane position, and first later-wave pressure.

subwindows.tile_cadence: 4 fps sheet uses 0.25 second cadence, left-to-right then top-to-bottom.
observations: Multiple attackers and projectiles appear in the lower and mid playfield.
Player movement visibly corrects under pressure.
Explosions and score changes are visible.
subwindows.implementation_use: Useful for later-wave dive density, player evasive movement, and shot/projectile vocabulary. First-pass frame-level Galaxip x trace now exists and should be manually spot-checked before tuning.

subwindows.tile_cadence: 2 fps sheet uses 0.5 second cadence, left-to-right then top-to-bottom.
observations: Formation is thinned while attackers continue from both sides and center lanes.
Player stays active across the lower field rather than holding a single lane.
Projectile and dive coverage spans enough of the screen to inform later-pressure hooks.
subwindows.implementation_use: Best current candidate for active gameplay pressure tracing and progression behavior.

subwindows.tile_cadence: 2 fps sheet uses 0.5 second cadence, left-to-right then top-to-bottom.
observations: Rack is heavily depleted and attack pressure continues with fewer enemies.
Player movement remains visible and reactive.
This window may help model late-wave cleanup rather than opening formation behavior.
subwindows.implementation_use: Useful for future progression and late-wave cleanup semantics; lower priority for the first scout-wave preview.

next_review_steps: Manually spot-check the early-attacks Galaxip x-position trace.
Generate frame-exact still sequences for mid-pressure.
Use this source as the active-gameplay complement to the Matt Hawkins presentation/rack source.
